title: Synaptic weight matrix (target rows, source columns)

purpose.canonicalQuestion: For a declared node universe drawn as target rows and source columns, and a declared snapshot time, what is the explicitly named aggregate of the synaptic weights of every observed connection in each cell?
cannotEstablish: That an empty cell is a connection whose weight is zero. An absent cell means no connection was observed under the declared scope. A measured zero weight is a drawn value with its own colour.

That a cell's colour describes one synapse. Unless `no_aggregation` is in force a cell is an aggregate over every multapse mapping to it, and the aggregate hides how many synapses and how much spread produced it.

That a net-zero cell contains no connection or only zero-valued ones. A sum of +8.0 and -8.0 is 0.0 — numerically identical to a single measured zero synapse. Only the contributing count and the min/max columns separate them.

The functional strength or sign of a connection's influence. A weight is a model parameter: in NEST it may act as a current amplitude, a conductance, or a dimensionless factor depending on the synapse and neuron model.
That the weights are current. This is a SNAPSHOT at the declared time. Under a plastic synapse model the weights at any other moment are different, and this figure says nothing about them.
Anything about connections held on another MPI rank under a rank-local scope, or about any node outside the declared node universe.
That the node ordering carries structure. The order is the caller's declared order; Cortexel never seriates or clusters, so an apparent block is only as real as the declared ordering.

normalization: Matrix orientation is FROZEN: a row is a TARGET (postsynaptic) node, a column is a SOURCE (presynaptic) node — NEST's convention. Rows and columns are both indexed by the ONE declared node universe. There is no transpose parameter and no caller-supplied axis label, because a transposed weight matrix is structurally indistinguishable from the original while inverting every directional claim the figure makes.

Row and column order is the caller's declared node-universe order. Cortexel NEVER seriates, clusters, or sorts rows or columns by any data-derived criterion. A reordered matrix manufactures visible block structure, and the reader has no way to tell a real module from an artifact of the sort.

Connection order from a recorder or a SynapseCollection is not assumed. Rows are stably sorted by (row index of target, column index of source, edge id, original ordinal) before aggregation. That canonical order is normative: binary64 addition is not associative, so the summation order is what makes `sum` byte-reproducible across the TypeScript and Python implementations.
Multapses are NEVER deduplicated. Two connection rows sharing source, target, weight, and model are two synapses; both contribute to the cell aggregate and to the contributing-connection count.

A null weight is a MISSING observation. It never contributes to an aggregate and is never read as zero. A cell whose every contributing connection has a null weight is `present_without_value`: not absent — the synapse exists — and not zero.
A weight of exactly 0 is a MEASURED value and is drawn as one. An absent cell carries a distinct legend key and is never painted as the colour scale's zero, its minimum, or its neutral midpoint.

Weights carry one quantity kind and one unit for the whole snapshot, in canonical codes only. `nest:weight` has dimension `simulator_defined`: it is never converted, never mapped to SI, and never pooled with any other code — including another simulator-defined code.

Negative zero is ACCEPTED, never rejected, and canonicalized to `0` under RFC 8785: -0.0 is a MEASURED zero weight and is drawn as one. It is never treated as a distinct value, and no error code exists for it. Cortexel applies no rounding, clamping, absolute value, or sign split to a weight at any point in the pipeline.
derivation: Cell key: (targetId, sourceId), both members of the declared node universe. Its contributing set is every connection row whose target and source match, taken in the canonical order. Only PRESENT cells are materialized: a 4,000-node universe carrying 50,000 connections costs 50,000 cells, not 16,000,000.

sum: the binary64 sum of the finite contributing weights, added in the canonical order. It is a NET. Summing +12.0 and -8.0 yields +4.0; summing +8.0 and -8.0 yields 0.0. Cancellation is invisible in the colour and is recoverable only from the contributing count and the min/max columns.
mean: sum / (number of contributing connections with a FINITE weight). The denominator is never the total contributing count when some weights are null, and it is never a cell count.
min / max: over the finite contributing weights. A tie reports the first contributing edge in the canonical order, so the reported edge id is deterministic rather than dependent on input order.

no_aggregation: ASSERTS that at most one connection maps to each cell. If any cell has two or more, the request FAILS with SCIENCE_AGGREGATION_REQUIRED. There is no implicit 'last edge wins' — it would silently discard a real synapse and can invert a cell's sign.

Colour domain, sequential: the extent of the finite aggregates. Colour domain, diverging: SYMMETRIC about the declared centre, [centre - M, centre + M] where M is the largest absolute deviation of any finite aggregate. An asymmetric diverging domain would give equal deviations of opposite sign unequal colour intensity.

A diverging scale additionally requires that the contributing WEIGHTS straddle the centre — at least one strictly below and one strictly above. This is a RENDER-stage rule (RENDER_DIVERGING_SCALE_NO_CENTER); the request-validation pipeline has no semantic validator for it (see knownLimitations).
Absent, unobserved, and present-without-value cells lie OUTSIDE the colour domain and each carries its own legend key. None of them is ever mapped to the minimum colour or to the diverging midpoint.
Complexity: O(e log e) for the canonical sort and O(e) for aggregation, in the connection count e. No step is quadratic in the node universe, and no dense row-by-column array is allocated.
scopeRules: The node (target/source) universe must be declared COMPLETE. An incomplete universe is REFUSED (SCOPE_NODE_UNIVERSE_REQUIRED): an edge list alone can only show that no connection was observed, never that a target has no incoming connection, so under `complete: false` no absent cell could be drawn as an absence.

A NetworkScopeV1 is mandatory and MUST carry `snapshotTime`. The shared scope type leaves `snapshotTime` optional; this figure requires it STRUCTURALLY (SCHEMA_REQUIRED_PROPERTY_MISSING when absent). A synaptic weight is a state variable: under a plastic synapse model the matrix at t = 0 and at t = 10,000 ms are different figures, and an undated weight matrix cannot be bound to a moment in the run.

Only `single_process`, `global_merged`, and a COMPLETE `mpi_target_rank_local` are admitted. The scope schema is NARROWED structurally, so an unsupported scope is refused as a schema error rather than reaching a scope-stage validator.

Scope `sampled` is REFUSED STRUCTURALLY (SCHEMA_ENUM_MISMATCH on `scope.kind`). Every cell value is an aggregate over the COMPLETE set of connections mapping to that cell. A sample does not contain that set, so no cell value is computable — not even for a cell whose edges were retained, because whether ITS multapses were fully retained is unknowable.

Scope `mpi_target_rank_local` with `localTargetUniverseComplete: false` is REFUSED STRUCTURALLY (SCHEMA_ENUM_MISMATCH): the schema requires `localTargetUniverseComplete: true`. Even a locally owned target's multapse set may be incomplete otherwise, so its aggregate would be an unquantified underestimate.

Scope `mpi_target_rank_local` with `localTargetUniverseComplete: true` is ACCEPTED. Under NEST's MPI semantics a rank holds every connection whose TARGET it owns, so any row with at least one present cell is necessarily a locally owned target and its cell aggregates are complete.

Under that same scope a row with NO present cell is ambiguous: either a target this rank does not own, or a locally owned target with no incoming connection. Cortexel cannot distinguish them, so it renders the entire row as `unobserved` and never as absent. It will not assert an absence the snapshot cannot support.

Scope `global_merged` must cover ranks 0..worldSize-1 exactly once (SCOPE_MERGE_INCOMPLETE). Only under that scope and under `single_process` does an absent cell carry the meaning 'no connection was observed'.

Every connection endpoint must be a member of the declared node universe. An out-of-universe connection is REFUSED (SEMANTIC_UNKNOWN_REFERENCE), never filtered — dropping it would paint an absent cell where a synapse exists.
The diagonal is never masked. An autapse occupies cell (i, i) and is aggregated and drawn exactly like any other connection.

A `delays` column may travel in the connection snapshot. This figure never analyses or draws it, and its presence can never change a cell value — but it is still validated: a nonpositive delay is REFUSED (SCIENCE_DELAY_NONPOSITIVE). A snapshot carrying a physically impossible delay is corrupt, and Cortexel validates every scientific column it accepts, not only the ones it draws.

nodeUniverse.description: The COMPLETE node universe, in the order it is drawn. Rows are TARGET (postsynaptic) nodes and columns are SOURCE (presynaptic) nodes — both indexed by this one universe. For a recurrent population autapses land on the diagonal. Must be complete: a target with no incoming connection is a scientific fact, and only a declared universe can carry it. Read by topology.node_universe_declared and topology.edge_endpoints_in_universe.

connections.description: The connection snapshot. `weights` is REQUIRED here — a weight matrix without weights is an adjacency matrix, and the two differ in how they treat an absent cell, a zero, and a repeated connection.

scope.description: The snapshot's scope. `snapshotTime` is REQUIRED here even though the shared type leaves it optional: a weight is a state variable, and an undated weight matrix cannot be bound to a moment in the run. The scope is additionally NARROWED — only `single_process`, `global_merged`, and a COMPLETE `mpi_target_rank_local` are admitted; a `sampled` scope and a rank-local scope with `localTargetUniverseComplete: false` are refused with a structural schema error, because a cell value is an aggregate over the COMPLETE connection set mapping to it and neither scope contains that set.

multapseAggregation.description: How multiple connections mapping to one cell are combined. Mandatory and closed. `no_aggregation` ASSERTS one connection per cell and fails with SCIENCE_AGGREGATION_REQUIRED if that is untrue; it is the only way to guarantee that a cell's colour describes a single synapse. Read by topology.multapse_aggregation_declared as parameters.multapseAggregation.

synapseModelGroup.description: Optional. Names a caller-declared comparability GROUP under which two or more distinct synapse models may be pooled onto one colour scale. When the snapshot carries two or more distinct `connections.synapseModels` and this is ABSENT, the request fails with SCIENCE_WEIGHT_GROUP_INCOMPATIBLE, because `nest:weight` is one code covering values whose physical meaning differs by model: the same number 10.0 may be 10 pA of postsynaptic current under one model and 10 nS of conductance under another. Its presence records the caller's comparability judgement; Cortexel verifies that the judgement was MADE, not that it is true, and (see knownLimitations) not that the group covers exactly the models present. Read by topology.weight_group_compatible as parameters.synapseModelGroup.

colorScale.description: The colour-map CLASS is a scientific declaration, not a style. A diverging map asserts that its centre is a meaningful neutral point and that deviations of opposite sign are comparable. Consumed by the figure.matrix renderer; the request-validation pipeline does not enforce the diverging straddle rule (see knownLimitations).

oneOf.description: An ordered-magnitude map (perceptually uniform, monotonic lightness). The correct choice whenever the weight domain has no meaningful neutral point — for example a strictly non-negative conductance weight.

A signed map about an explicit centre. At render time it is refused with RENDER_DIVERGING_SCALE_NO_CENTER unless the contributing weights actually straddle the centre — at least one strictly below and one strictly above it.

center.description: The neutral value. For an excitatory/inhibitory weight domain this is 0: no net drive. There is no default, because a centre chosen by the library would be a scientific claim the library is not entitled to make.

uncertainty.description: Required, and only the `none` variant is supported. A heatmap cell is one colour: there is no visual channel left for a band, so any other variant is REFUSED (SCIENCE_UNCERTAINTY_UNSUPPORTED_FOR_SKILL) rather than silently discarded. Within-cell spread is reachable through the contributing count and the min/max table columns.

accessibility.summaryTemplate: Synaptic weight matrix over a declared node universe of {nodeCount} nodes; rows are targets (postsynaptic), columns are sources (presynaptic). {valuedCellCount} cells carry a weight aggregate, {presentWithoutValueCellCount} hold connections whose weight is missing, {absentCellCount} have no observed connection, {unobservedCellCount} were not observed under this scope. Aggregate: {aggregation} over {connectionCount} connections in {weightUnit}, from {aggregateMin} to {aggregateMax}. Comparability: {synapseModelGroupStatement}. Colour scale: {colorScaleStatement}. Scope: {scopeStatement} at {snapshotTime}. {multapseStatement} {compactionStatement}

tableColumns.description: The postsynaptic node. Rows are targets; this is fixed by the contract and is not caller-configurable.
The presynaptic node. Null on an unobserved-row record, where the record stands for the entire row.

valued | present_without_value | absent | unobserved. Absent means no connection was observed under a scope that can support an absence claim. Unobserved means the scope cannot support one. Neither is a zero.
The named aggregate of the contributing weights, to full binary64 precision. Empty when the cell is not valued.
The declared formula. There is no default and no 'last edge wins'.
A `nest:weight` unit is simulator-defined: it has no SI mapping and is never converted or compared across systems.
How many connection rows map to this cell. Greater than 1 means the colour is an aggregate over multapses, not a synapse.
How many of those connections had a finite weight. This is the denominator of `mean`; it differs from the connection count when any weight is missing.
The smallest contributing weight. With the max and the count, this is the only route to the spread the single cell colour conceals.
The largest contributing weight. A cell whose min is negative and whose max is positive contains cancelling synapses.
The distinct declared models contributing to the cell, and the caller-declared comparability group (synapseModelGroup) under which they were pooled, if any.

The stable ids of every contributing connection, or a digest-bound sidecar reference to them. Declared edge ids are preserved; absent ones become deterministic ordinals assigned after the canonical sort.
The snapshot scope and time this cell was observed under.

externalOracle.name: scipy.sparse.coo_matrix duplicate summation, cross-checked against a NEST GetConnections weight snapshot
externalOracle.notes: coo_matrix sums duplicate (row, col) entries when converted to CSR or dense, which makes it an exact differential oracle for `aggregation: sum` and for NOTHING else: it has no mean, min, max, or no_aggregation semantics, and it maps an absent cell to 0.0 — the precise conflation this contract exists to prevent. The comparison is therefore valid only on the present cells and only under `sum`, and the absent-cell handling must be excluded before it is meaningful. Orientation must be pinned explicitly (row = target, col = source) because coo_matrix takes (row, col) positionally and a transposed oracle would agree on every symmetric fixture. The pinned reference environment has NOT been executed here, so the status is not_run rather than assumed.

adapters.notes: GetConnections plus get(['source', 'target', 'weight', 'synapse_model']). The adapter must additionally supply what a SynapseCollection cannot express: the complete node universe (an empty target row is invisible to a connection query), the scope, and the snapshot time. It accepts the official singular/scalar form or canonical plural arrays, never both; it never broadcasts a scalar weight across rows; and it never deduplicates multapses. Accessor-bearing input is rejected before any getter can run.

Produces `mpi_target_rank_local`. Accepted only with localTargetUniverseComplete: true, and then every entirely empty target row renders as `unobserved` rather than absent. The adapter must not merge ranks implicitly: a merged claim requires every rank and is checked.

adapters.system: connectome edge table (source, target, weight, model)
adapters.notes: Accepted only when the table is accompanied by the complete node universe, a scope, a snapshot time, and a declared weight quantity. A bare edge table cannot establish an isolate, a target with no input, or the difference between an absent cell and a measured zero.

Not an input. A COO/CSR/dense matrix has already destroyed the facts this contract requires: duplicate entries were summed (or not) under no recorded policy, an absent cell and a zero weight have both become 0.0, and the contributing edge identities are gone. A matrix is an OUTPUT of this figure, never an input to it.
Weight arrays are per-synapse and multapses are representable, so the mapping is mechanical. It is not certified until a version matrix and fixtures exist.

migration.notes: A multapse aggregation is now MANDATORY: the legacy payload had no declared policy for repeated connections mapping to one cell, so its cells were whatever the last write happened to be. Migration also REQUIRES a weight quantity with a declared dimension, a complete node universe, a network scope, and a snapshot time; without any of these it BLOCKS with MIGRATION_INFORMATION_MISSING rather than guessing. The legacy `nest.connectivity_matrix` does not migrate here automatically — it is blocked with MIGRATION_AMBIGUOUS_CONNECTIVITY_MATRIX and the caller must choose adjacency, weight, or delay, because the three differ in exactly the absence, zero, and aggregation semantics a guess would get wrong.

changePolicy: Changing the target-row / source-column orientation is MAJOR. It inverts every directional claim in every stored artifact while leaving the figure structurally identical.
Changing what an absent, unobserved, or present-without-value cell means is MAJOR.
Changing an aggregation formula or its denominator — in particular the `mean` denominator when weights are missing — is MAJOR.
Changing the canonical connection sort order is MAJOR: it changes the binary64 summation order for `sum` and therefore the artifact digest.
Changing which scopes are legal, or relaxing the snapshot-time requirement, is MAJOR.
Changing the diverging straddle rule or the symmetric diverging domain is MAJOR.
Adding a new aggregation id to the registry and accepting it here is MINOR.
Adding a transpose, a clustering/seriation option, a colour-domain clamp, or a diagonal mask would be MAJOR, and each is refused outright today.
knownLimitations: This figure models the weight matrix over a SINGLE declared node universe: rows are targets and columns are sources, both indexed by that universe (a recurrent population's square matrix; a disjoint source/target set is expressed by listing the union). A bipartite projection with two separately declared, differently ordered universes is not offered, because the topology validators read one `data.nodeUniverse`, and a two-universe shape would leave endpoint-in-universe and universe-completeness unenforced. Registry gap.

The diverging colour-domain straddle rule — a diverging scale requires contributing weights on both sides of the centre — is enforced only at the RENDER stage (RENDER_DIVERGING_SCALE_NO_CENTER). The request-validation pipeline has no semantic validator for it, so no living request-level invalid fixture asserts it. Registry gap.

`count_weighted_mean` is named conditionally in the blueprint but is not defined in the multapse-aggregation registry, so it is not offered. Registry gap: it needs a precise definition before any implementation may accept it.

`synapseModelGroup` records a caller-declared comparability claim. Cortexel verifies only that the group was DECLARED when two or more distinct synapse models are present (topology.weight_group_compatible checks presence, not content). It cannot verify that the declared group's model set exactly matches the models in the snapshot, nor that two synapse models' weights are actually comparable — both stay attributed to the caller. No registered disclosure id carries the claim, so it is surfaced through the accessible summary and the synapse-model table column. Registry gap.
A `nest:weight` value has no SI meaning. Cortexel never converts, compares, or pools it across simulators, and it cannot check that the number means what the caller believes.

Under `mpi_target_rank_local` Cortexel cannot enumerate the rank's local target set, so an entirely empty row is reported as unobserved. This is conservative: it never asserts an absence it cannot support, and it therefore never confirms one either.
No colour-domain clamping parameter is offered, because no registered disclosure could carry a saturated-cell fact. The domain is always the extent of the finite aggregates.
Within-cell dispersion is not drawn. A cell is one colour; the spread of its contributing weights is reachable only through the count and min/max table columns.
Axis tick labels are thinned above a bounded row/column count. Thinning a LABEL never removes a cell: every row and column stays individually addressable in the table and the sidecar.
The matrix is materialized sparsely. A dense export of a node universe beyond the profile's matrix-cell limit is refused rather than streamed.

invalid.why: `no_aggregation` asserts one connection per cell, but two connections map to (t1, s1). Under an implicit 'last edge wins' the cell would be painted -8.0 pA and read as INHIBITORY, when the net of the two synapses is +4.0 pA — excitatory. The sign of the claim inverts, so Cortexel refuses instead of picking a winner by array order.

A connection targets `t9`, which is not in the declared node universe. Filtering it — the common convenience — would leave cell (t9, s1) undrawn and every other cell unchanged, so the figure would report an absence for a synapse that exists. Cortexel refuses rather than silently narrowing a universe the caller declared complete.

Two synapse models are pooled with no declared comparability group, and the unit is the simulator-defined `nest:weight`. Both rows read 10.0, but a static_synapse weight onto a current-based iaf_psc_alpha is 10 pA of postsynaptic current while an stdp_synapse weight onto a conductance-based cell is 10 nS. Their `sum` is 20.0 — a number that is not a current, not a conductance, and not anything. Only a declared synapseModelGroup can pool them; absent it, Cortexel refuses.

A standard deviation is supplied for the cell aggregate. A heatmap cell has exactly one colour and no second visual channel, so the dispersion could only be silently discarded: this cell aggregates 8 multapses spanning 0.1 to 20.0 pA with a mean of 5.0 and an SD of 7.0, and it would be drawn identically to a single 5.0 pA synapse. Cortexel refuses the variant instead, and routes the spread to the count and min/max table columns where it survives.

A sampled scope cannot produce a weight matrix at all. Every cell value is an aggregate over the COMPLETE set of connections mapping to that cell, and a sample of 5,000 of 50,000 connections does not contain it: a cell whose true sum is 40.0 pA across 4 synapses may retain 1 and be drawn as 10.0 pA. This figure's schema does not admit a `sampled` scope, so the request is refused structurally on `scope.kind` rather than being drawn as an unquantified underestimate.

The scope carries no snapshot time. A synaptic weight is a state variable: under stdp_synapse the same network's matrix at t = 0 and at t = 10,000 ms are different figures, and a stored artifact with no snapshot time can be re-read later as evidence for whichever moment suits. The shared scope type leaves snapshotTime optional; this figure requires it structurally, because a weight matrix without one cannot be bound to a moment in the run.

The node universe declares `t1` twice. A duplicate node id makes the cell a connection lands in ambiguous, and the ambiguity would silently diverge the drawn matrix, the selection, and the table — the same connection can be counted into row 0 by one component and row 1 by another. Identity must fail before any binding can happen.

`nanosiemens` is an accepted alias, not the canonical code `nS`. A stable request must use the canonical code: a silent alias conversion changes a number the caller never sees, and on a weight — the one quantity whose dimension may be simulator-defined — an unnoticed factor is exactly the class of error this contract exists to stop.

Scope `mpi_target_rank_local` is declared with `localTargetUniverseComplete: false`. Even for a target this rank owns, the multapse set may be truncated, so the cell aggregate would be an unquantified underestimate. This figure's schema requires `localTargetUniverseComplete: true`, so the request is refused structurally on that field rather than being drawn as a shortfall the figure cannot reveal.

A `global_merged` scope declares worldSize 2 but merges only rank 0. The result is a rank-local snapshot wearing a global label: every connection whose target rank 1 owns is missing, so its rows would be drawn as absent — an assertion that no connection was observed, made about data that was never collected. A merge is global only when it covers 0..worldSize-1 exactly once.

The node universe is declared incomplete. Every absent cell in the matrix then means 'no connection was observed among the nodes I happened to list', not 'no connection exists' — and the two are drawn identically. A weight matrix's absent cell is a scientific claim, so it requires a universe that can support one.

A `delays` column travels alongside the weights and carries -1.5 ms. This figure never draws a delay, and it would be easy to let the column through untouched — but a negative transmission delay is physically impossible, so the snapshot is corrupt, and a corrupt snapshot cannot be trusted to have produced sound weights either. Cortexel validates every scientific column it accepts, not only the ones it draws.

The weights are declared `synaptic_weight` but carry the unit `ms`, whose dimension is time. A weight is not a duration. The numbers would otherwise be summed onto a colour scale labelled in milliseconds — a matrix of aggregated delays presented as synaptic strength — and every value in it would be structurally well-formed and scientifically meaningless.

Two connections are declared but only one weight. Pairing by best effort would bind weight 12.0 to the first connection and silently leave the second weightless — or, worse under a shifted read, bind 12.0 to the wrong (target, source) cell entirely. A parallel array is a positional claim, so a length mismatch is an unresolvable ambiguity about which synapse the number belongs to.

The request tries to assert that Cortexel validated it. Validation results are library-generated facts and are not caller-settable; the field is rejected, not ignored. A caller declares what its data IS, never what Cortexel concluded about it.
